stories.title: Iran-U.S. ceasefire off to a shaky start. And, Bill Gates to testify in Epstein probe
stories.description: Attacks persist across the Middle East despite the two-week ceasefire deal between the U.S. and Iran. And, Bill Gates is set to testify in the investigation of the late sex offender Jeffrey Epstein.

stories.title: Trump warns strikes will resume if Iran doesn't agree to his peace terms
stories.description: President Trump said that any peace deal would not allow nuclear enrichment in Iran, and would need to keep the Strait of Hormuz open, as conflicting messages surface over the terms of the ceasefire.

stories.title: Morning news brief
stories.description: Trump and NATO chief meet to discuss the partnership, attacks continue across the Gulf region despite U.S.-Iran ceasefire, Iran says ceasefire includes Lebanon, but Israel and Trump say it's not.

stories.title: What has the U.S. war with Iran accomplished?
stories.description: Trump's war goals included putting an end to Iran's nuclear program, destroying its military capabilities and creating regime change. That hasn't happened.

stories.title: Lebanon thought there was a ceasefire - then Israel unleashed deadly blitz
stories.description: Israel says Lebanon is not included in the ceasefire the US agreed to halt the war with Iran.

stories.title: Israeli strikes in Lebanon 'grave violation' of ceasefire, Iran minister tells BBC
stories.description: Iran's deputy foreign minister says the US must choose between 'war and ceasefire'.

stories.title: Has US achieved its war objectives in Iran?
stories.description: Key US objectives at the start of the war were to stop Iran getting a nuclear weapon and degrading its arsenal.

stories.title: Negotiators face huge task to close gaps in rival Iran peace proposals
stories.description: The US has a 15-point plan to end the war, with Iran offering 10 proposals of its own. Can they be reconciled?

stories.title: At least 182 killed across Lebanon in large wave of Israeli strikes
stories.description: Attacks hit the southern suburbs of Beirut, southern Lebanon and the eastern Bekaa Valley, hours after a US-Iran ceasefire was announced.

stories.title: Israel backs US-Iran ceasefire but Netanyahu's war goals remain unfulfilled
stories.description: The Israeli prime minister's response to the ceasefire has been muted, in contrast to the triumphal statements from the US and Iran.

stories.title: Why ceasefire deal with US has unsettled Iran's hardliners
stories.description: The two-week truce opens up the prospect of direct talks with the US but has angered Iran's hardliners, writes BBC News Persian’s Kasra Naji.

stories.title: Ships remain cautious approaching Strait of Hormuz amid fragile ceasefire
stories.description: Only a few vessels have crossed the strait since the US-Iran ceasefire deal, according to BBC Verify analysis.

stories.title: Oil prices plunge and shares jump on US-Iran ceasefire plan
stories.description: Crude prices tumbled by as much as 15% on the conditional pause but remain far higher than before the war.

stories.title: French couple leave Iran after more than three years in jail
stories.description: Cecile Kohler and Jacques Paris are on their way home after being allowed to leave the country, the French president says.

stories.title: Iran ceasefire deal gives Trump a way out of war - but at a high cost
stories.description: The path to the two-week ceasefire with Iran may have fundamentally altered the way the rest of the world views the US.

stories.title: What we know about the two-week ceasefire between the US and Iran
stories.description: The provisional truce comes more than a month after the US and Israel launched coordinated attacks on Iran.

stories.title: How Pakistan helped secure a fragile ceasefire between the US and Iran
stories.description: Pakistan has a historic relationship with Iran, but an agreement was far from certain, writes the BBC’s Caroline Davies

stories.title: Little sign of breakthrough as Trump's Iran deadline nears
stories.description: The US president is in a delicate political position as the final hours to Tuesday's deadline for Iran to open the Strait of Hormuz tick down.

stories.title: How rescue of US airman in remote part of Iran unfolded
stories.description: The operation to extract him from the ground in hostile territory was hugely complex and involved multiple US government agencies.

stories.title: Israel dismisses calls to add Lebanon to US-Iran ceasefire
stories.description: Israel vowed more strikes against Hezbollah on Thursday, dismissing mounting international demands that the fragile truce between the United States and Iran in the Gulf be expanded to cover the war in Lebanon. At least 203 people were killed and 1,000 wounded in the latest strike...

stories.title: Iranians rally to mark 40-day mourning for slain supreme leader Khamenei
stories.description: Mourners across Iran began ceremonies Thursday marking the 40th day after the death of Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, who was killed at the start of the Iran war. In Iran’s capital, Tehran, mourners wearing black began their rally from Jomhouri Eslami Square to the neighb...

stories.title: Taking stock of Iran war after 40 days of conflict
stories.description: As global attention turns to the fragile ceasefire agreed between Iran and the United States, the 40 days of conflict have left lasting scars across Iran – with significant damage to infrastructure and military capabilities, even as Tehran claims victory. In Washington, the Trump...

stories.title: India hit by Middle East war: Millions face gas shortages
stories.description: In the weeks leading up to the ceasefire, the impact of the war in the Middle East was felt far beyond the region, reaching countries such as India. The nation of nearly 1.5 billion people relies heavily on imports of cooking gas, or LPG, much of it sourced from the Middle East. ...

stories.title: Rising oil prices: Are we heading for a recession?
stories.description: Crude oil prices rose again on Thursday as the ceasefire announced by US President Donald Trump and Iran shows signs of fragility. In this edition, we speak with Ilian Mihov, Professor of Economics at INSEAD, about whether the war and the resulting oil shock could trigger a globa...

stories.title: Cannes 2026 Palme d’Or picks include Iran’s Farhadi, Spain’s Almodovar
stories.description: The list of 21 directors vying for the Palme d’Or at the 2026 Cannes Film Festival includes world cinema auteurs such as Iran’s Asghar Farhadi and Spain's Pedro Almodovar, organisers revealed on Thursday. Major Hollywood studios are set to be notable in their absence on the Frenc...

stories.title: 'Black Wednesday': Lebanon declares day of mourning amid ongoing Israeli attacks
stories.description: Lebanon declared a national day of mourning on Thursday after Israeli strikes pummelled the country, killing nearly 200 people and calling into question the ceasefire between the United States and Iran. FRANCE 24's Renée Davis reports from Beirut, Lebanon.

stories.title: Chart shows Iran may have put sea mines in Strait of Hormuz
stories.description: Semiofficial news agencies in Iran published a chart Thursday suggesting the country’s paramilitary Revolutionary Guard put sea mines into the Strait of Hormuz during the war, in a message that may be intended to pressure the US as uncertainty hangs over a days-old two-week cease...

stories.title: US-Iran truce hangs in balance as war flares in Lebanon
stories.description: The fragile two-week truce between Iran and the United States was hanging in the balance on Wednesday, with Tehran threatening to resume hostilities as Israel launched a major bombardment of Lebanon. At least 182 people were killed and nearly 900 wounded on Wednesday, the Lebanes...

stories.title: US–Iran ceasefire: Has Iran’s regime emerged weakened or reinforced?
stories.description: US President Donald Trump declared the war a complete success following the ceasefire. Iran, despite significant damage to infrastructure and its military, also claimed victory. Even though weakened and demoralised, has the Iranian regime emerged strengthened from these 40 days o...

stories.title: What is Iran’s Strait of Hormuz protocol and will other nations accept it?
stories.description: Tehran's plans to tax ships passing through the strait and raise money to rebuild is already seeing pushback.

stories.title: The economic cost of US-Israel war on Iran
stories.description: The economic cost of US-Israel war on Iran

stories.title: US-Iran talks in Pakistan: Who’s attending, what’s on the agenda?
stories.description: Islamabad hosts negotiations as US and Iran seek off-ramp. But deep mistrust and regional tensions threaten talks.

stories.title: Lebanon Latest: Israel kills hundreds in one day
stories.description: Israel launches a wave of strikes in Lebanon that has killed hundreds, despite a US-Iran ceasefire.

stories.title: Why Israel’s attacks on Lebanon could cripple US-Iran ceasefire
stories.description: Ceasefire dispute escalates as US and Israel exclude Lebanon, raising fears of a broader regional conflict.

stories.title: Several people reported killed in fresh Israeli attacks on Lebanon
stories.description: Lebanon bears the brunt and US-Iran truce at risk as Israel claims killing of a close aide to Hezbollah's leader.

stories.title: The war on Iran: Nobody won, everyone paid
stories.description: The US-Israeli campaign has failed to achieve its goals. Iran has been badly hit, and the Gulf is paying the bill too.

stories.title: Can global supply chains recover from the Iran war?
stories.description: Conflict upends flow of critical raw materials for manufacturing, aviation and technology.

stories.title: Iran has been bloodied, but it is winning against the US-Israel axis
stories.description: The US and Israel thought they could decimate Iran. But Iran's survival - despite massive human costs - is a victory.

stories.title: Trump says US forces to stay near Iran, ready for ‘next conquest’
stories.description: US threats against Iran and ongoing Israeli attacks in Lebanon cast doubt on fragile ceasefire efforts.

stories.title: Iran, Saudi FMs hold first call since start of conflict, discuss ways to reduce tensions
stories.description: Saudi Foreign Minister Prince Faisal bin Farhan received a phone call from his Iranian counterpart Abbas Araghchi, the Saudi foreign ministry said on Thursday. It marked the first official contact between the countries since Tehran launched strikes against its Gulf neighbours in ...

stories.title: Lebanon seeks Pakistan’s support for ending Israeli strikes
stories.description: Lebanon’s Prime Minister Nawaf Salam held a phone call with Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif on Thursday, in which he sought Pakistan’s support for bringing an immediate end to Israeli attacks on his country, according to the Prime Minister’s Office (PMO). The statement said that du...

stories.title: India election roll overhaul sparks anger as key states vote
stories.description: A key opposition Indian politician has threatened legal action against the election commission after millions of people were struck off electoral rolls, the latest flashpoint over a contentious voter clean-up drive. A sweeping voter registration overhaul — known as the Special In...

stories.title: IMF strikes staff-level deal with Sri Lanka on $700m in funding
stories.description: The International Monetary Fund (IMF) reached a staff-level pact with Sri Lanka, which will unlock financing of about $700 million once approved, the lender said on Thursday, calling for reforms, including in fuel levies, to ensure stability and growth. The deal comes as the isla...

stories.title: Pakistan’s envoy to US acknowledges China’s quiet but persuasive role in Iran talks
stories.description: WASHINGTON: Pakistan’s mediation that helped bring about a temporary US–Iran ceasefire has drawn global attention, with Islamabad’s envoy to Washington reiterating that the breakthrough was the result of “an intense diplomatic effort that was in progress over the past few weeks.”...

stories.title: Foolproof security arranged for foreign guests, Naqvi assures US envoy ahead of US‑Iran talks in Islamabad
stories.description: Interior Minister Mohsin Naqvi on Thursday assured US Ambassador to Pakistan Natalie Baker of foolproof security for all foreign dignitaries ahead of the US-Iran talks scheduled in Islamabad this week. Pakistan is set to host in-person talks between the US and Iran on Saturday af...

stories.title: Trump declares end to Iran’s uranium enrichment
stories.description: • Vows joint push to ‘dig up, remove’ buried nuclear material; threatens 50pc tariffs on nations supplying weapons to Tehran• Hegseth says US will ‘take’ uranium if Iran refuses to hand it over WASHINGTON: President Don­ald Trump said Wednesday there will be no enrichment of uran...

stories.title: Islamabad takes centre stage, to host high-stakes US-Iran talks
stories.description: • Negotiations follow Pakistan-brokered two-week truce• US vice president, Iranian foreign minister to lead their delegations• PM Shehbaz calls talks ‘historic moment’ for lasting settlement• Iran warns of withdrawal from ceasefire if violations continue• US says truce excludes L...

stories.title: 15 nations plan to help restore traffic through Hormuz: Macron
stories.description: French President Emmanuel Macron welcomes his counterpart from Ghana, John Dramani Mahama, to the Elysee Palace.—AFP • President says France to lead mission for securing key oil route after US-Iran truce• First ships cross strategic strait; major shipping lines hold back, citing ...

stories.title: US urged to end Ukraine war after Iran truce
stories.description: • Ukranian FM says ‘American decisiveness’ in Gulf can be replicated• Kremlin also signals hope for peace talks• US vice president calls conflict ‘hardest to solve’, pledges continued effort KYIV: Kyiv on Wednesday called on the United States to pressure Russia into ending its in...

stories.title: World Bank cuts Middle East growth forecast after energy sector turmoil
stories.description: DUBAI: The World Bank has slashed its 2026 growth forecast for Middle East economies as a consequence of the US-Israel war against Iran, and warned of pervasive risks in a report published on Wednesday. President Donald Tru­mp late on Tuesday announced a two-week ceasefire in the...

stories.title: Israel pounds Lebanon with heaviest airstrikes of war as Hezbollah pauses attacks
stories.description: Israel carried out its heaviest strikes on Lebanon since the conflict with Hezbollah broke out last month, even as the Iran-aligned group paused attacks on northern Israel and Israeli troops in Lebanon under a two-week US-Iran ceasefire. Consecutive explosions shook Beirut, sendi...

stories.title: China, Russia veto UN resolution to reopen Strait of Hormuz
stories.description: WASHINGTON: Russia and China on Tuesday vetoed a draft resolution at the United Nations Security Council for reopening the Strait of Hormuz, while Pakistan abstained. The draft resolution prepared by Bahrain, which underwent six revisions during negotiations to bridge differences...

stories.title: First ships pass through Strait of Hormuz since ceasefire: monitor
stories.description: Two ships have passed through the Strait of Hormuz since Iran agreed to reopen the waterway as part of a ceasefire deal, maritime monitor Marine Traffic said on Wednesday. “The Greek-owned bulk carrier NJ Earth crossed the Strait at 08:44 UTC, while the Liberia-flagged Daytona Be...

stories.title: North Korea fires two rounds of ballistic missiles: Seoul military
stories.description: North Korea fired multiple short-range ballistic missiles in two rounds on Wednesday, South Korea’s military said, after reporting an “unidentified projectile” was launched from the Pyongyang area the previous day. South Korean President Lee Jae Myung has sought to repair ties wi...

stories.title: Iran-Israel war LIVE: Iran's President Pezeshkian says Israel's strikes on Lebanon render negotiations 'meaningless'
stories.description: The truce showed signs of unravelling, with Israel bombarding Lebanon and Washington contradicting some of Iran's demands to end the war ahead of planned talks

stories.title: Press review: Europe seeks to block US-Russia thaw as Russia eyes role in US-Iran talks
stories.description: Top stories from the Russian press on Friday, June 6th

stories.title: Israel to continue striking Beirut until Lebanon disarms Hezbollah — defense minister
stories.description: "Agreements must be honored and the Lebanese government does not do what is required, Israel will continue to act, and with great force," Israel Katz said

stories.title: Orban sees EU bracing for another decade of countering Russia amid proxy war in Ukraine
stories.description: Commenting on the Ukraine crisis, the Hungarian PM said that Brussels viewed Ukraine as "the first line of European defense" where pan-European interests were currently being defended

stories.title: Israel strikes Hezbollah targets in Beirut suburbs — IDF
stories.description: Prior to that, the Israeli army issued a warning for this neighborhood, telling residents to leave their homes immediately

stories.title: DPRK slams U.S. for orchestrating efforts to tighten financial sanctions against Pyongyang
stories.description: The Democratic People's Republic of Korea (DPRK) has slammed the United States for orchestrating efforts to tighten financial sanctions against Pyongyang.
